title: The Dollars and Sense of the New Electronic Commerce: Business-to-Business Electronic Commerce in Transition
author: Aberdeen Group (David Alschuler)
source_file: 1998 The Dollars and Sense of the New Electronic Commerce- Business-to-Business Electronic Commerce in Transition.pdf
abstract: This 115-page Aberdeen Group report by analyst David Alschuler examines the transition in B2B electronic commerce from traditional EDI/EFT systems to internet-based commerce platforms. The study profiles 17 supplier organizations within an analytical framework of EC capabilities, covering the full technology spectrum from EDI VANs through emerging internet commerce platforms, and addresses the strategic implications for corporate management responsible for e-commerce, supply chain, and technology planning. It introduced the concept of Infinite Resource Planning as the promise of the new EC.
importance_rationale: This report documented the pivotal 1998 EDI-to-internet transition in B2B commerce at the moment it was occurring, providing the first comprehensive Aberdeen framework for evaluating the nascent internet EC market; it was published at the inflection point before the dot-com boom and the rise of companies like Ariba and Commerce One.
relevance_rationale: The analytical framework distinguishing traditional EDI from internet-based EC remains historically instructive for understanding the arc of B2B commerce standardization; the transition described (EDI to XML/web services to APIs) continued and is directly ancestral to modern EDI/API integration platforms.
prescience_rationale: The report accurately predicted that internet commerce would displace EDI as the primary B2B channel; that the market would see notable successes and failures (Commerce One went bankrupt 2004; Ariba was acquired by SAP 2012); and that trading partner integration complexity would be the central challenge — all proven correct.
